wrapUp: France and Morocco are the first confirmed quarter-final pairing after Kylian Mbappe's penalty edged Les Bleus past Paraguay in extreme Philadelphia heat and Azzedine Ounahi's double helped Morocco eliminate co-hosts Canada. The next bracket swing comes on 5 July, with Brazil facing a Norway side built around Erling Haaland and Mexico trying to use the Azteca altitude against England. Argentina survived Cape Verde's scare but now meet Egypt, who advanced on penalties through Mohamed Salah and a perfect shoot-out. Morocco's Ismael Saibari injury and Brazil's expected absence of Lucas Paqueta are the key fitness notes shaping the next round. Each story below links to the specific report it is drawn from; squad and fitness details reflect the latest available reporting as of 5 July 2026.

sections.heading: Injuries and fitness doubts
sections.description: Players ruled out, late replacements and day-to-day concerns that could change starting line-ups and squad depth.

stories.headline: Paqueta expected to miss Brazil's Norway test
stories.summary: AP reports Lucas Paqueta is not expected to play against Norway after limping off at half-time in Brazil's Round of 32 win over Japan with a hamstring injury. Carlo Ancelotti said Brazil do not have another player with Paqueta's exact characteristics, so the midfield balance around Bruno Guimaraes and the supply into Vinicius Junior becomes a tactical question against an organised Norway side built around Erling Haaland.

stories.headline: Quansah available but James still a doubt for Mexico
stories.summary: Thomas Tuchel told BBC Sport that Jarell Quansah trained fully and is available for England's last-16 tie with Mexico, while Reece James still needs a final medical assessment and may only make the bench. The right-back decision matters because Julian Quinones has three goals at this World Cup and Mexico have started quickly at altitude in the Estadio Azteca.

stories.headline: Saibari worry follows Morocco into France quarter-final
stories.summary: Ismael Saibari was forced off after 22 minutes of Morocco's 3-0 win over Canada and was replaced by Soufiane Rahimi, who later scored the stoppage-time third. ESPN and Sky Sports both reported the early change, leaving Morocco to wait on their leading attacking option before the 9 July quarter-final against France in Foxborough.

sections.heading: Squad selection and tactical reshuffles
sections.description: Omissions, late call-ups and manager decisions that reveal how teams plan to navigate the expanded format.

stories.headline: Brazil's structure faces Haaland and Norway history
stories.summary: Brazil face Norway in East Rutherford on 5 July, with AP describing Ancelotti's side as slight favourites but noting Norway's unbeaten history in four meetings with Brazil. Haaland has five goals at the tournament and 25 in his past 14 competitive internationals, while Brazil's forward threat rests on Vinicius Junior and Matheus Cunha after a less fluent knockout opener against Japan.

stories.headline: England adjust for Azteca altitude and Mexico's fast starts
stories.summary: England face Mexico at 01:00 BST on 6 July with Tuchel warning that the squad cannot fully adapt to the altitude and must survive the hosts' early pressure. BBC Sport notes Mexico have not conceded at home during this tournament, while AP highlights El Tri's three wins at the Azteca and the home-field edge that could shape England's tempo and selection.

stories.headline: Argentina's scare sets up Salah's Egypt test
stories.summary: Argentina needed extra time and a Diney Borges own goal to beat Cape Verde 3-2 after the tournament debutants equalised twice, including Sidny Lopes Cabral's 103rd-minute strike. Egypt reached the same last-16 path by beating Australia 4-2 on penalties after a 1-1 draw, with Mohamed Salah converting his spot kick, so Lionel Scaloni's side now face a side with belief and a clear penalty pathway in Atlanta on 7 July.

stories.headline: Ounahi and Rahimi show Morocco's depth after slow start
stories.summary: Morocco were second best for much of the first half against Canada, but Azzedine Ounahi scored twice after the break and substitute Soufiane Rahimi added a third in stoppage time. The reshuffle after Saibari's injury gives Mohamed Ouahbi a selection question before facing France, but the Canada result also showed Morocco can win knockout matches without controlling long spells.

sections.heading: Disruption and preparation issues
sections.description: Travel, visa and logistical problems that could affect match readiness rather than just headlines.

stories.headline: Two last-16 ties define the 5 July bracket
stories.summary: AP's 5 July schedule has Brazil vs Norway in East Rutherford at 4 p.m. EDT and Mexico vs England at the Estadio Azteca at 8 p.m. EDT, with the winners due to meet in a quarter-final outside Miami on 11 July. The timing means England and Mexico will know whether Brazil or Norway await before they kick off, while Brazil must solve Norway without the expected availability of Paqueta.

stories.headline: France survive heat and physical Paraguay approach
stories.summary: France's 1-0 win over Paraguay was played under an extreme heat warning with temperatures around 38C, and ESPN reported that Paraguay's defensive approach kept the match tight until Mbappe's 70th-minute penalty. AP detailed repeated flashpoints around Mbappe and post-match tension, making recovery and discipline part of France's short lead-in to Morocco.

sections.heading: Analysis and tournament outlook
sections.description: Deeper previews on favourites, group dynamics and how the 48-team format changes the path to the final.

stories.headline: Sunday winners feed into a loaded Miami quarter-final
stories.summary: AP's 5 July schedule confirms Brazil vs Norway and Mexico vs England as the two remaining Sunday last-16 ties, with the winners due to meet outside Miami on 11 July. That path creates a sharp contrast with the confirmed France vs Morocco quarter-final on the other side of the bracket, and keeps England, Mexico, Brazil and Norway in the same high-variance lane.

stories.headline: Cape Verde scare leaves Argentina with warning before Egypt
stories.summary: Argentina advanced 3-2 after extra time against Cape Verde, but Sky Sports described the performance as laboured and Cape Verde's two equalisers as evidence of how close the champions came to a historic upset. Messi scored the opener and Argentina generated enough pressure to survive, yet Egypt's penalty win over Australia means the next tie has the same upset ingredients if Scaloni's side leave transition gaps.

stories.headline: Mbappe keeps France on course but Paraguay expose friction
stories.summary: Kylian Mbappe's 70th-minute penalty was enough for France to beat Paraguay 1-0, his 19th World Cup goal in 19 appearances and seventh of this tournament. ESPN reported France will now face Morocco in Foxborough, but the narrow scoreline, heat and physical nature of the match give Didier Deschamps tactical questions before meeting a side that has already removed Canada and the Netherlands.

stories.headline: Morocco reach second straight quarter-final with counter-attacking edge
stories.summary: Morocco beat Canada 3-0 despite a difficult first half, with Bounou making early saves before Ounahi and Rahimi punished Canada's stretched shape after the break. BBC analysis framed Morocco as unbeaten in 34 matches and a serious contender, while ESPN's match report shows a side capable of changing a game without sustained territorial control.
